programme.title: George Clarkes Old House, New Home
programme.desc: George squeezes into an Edwardian Workers Cottage which is only half the size of a squash court. This dolls house is home to NHS theatre nurse Hannah and her two young children. George has his work cut out for him as he tries to make each inch work.

programme.title: Matt Baker: Travels with Mum and Dad
programme.desc: The Bakers head to the World Heritage listed Durham Cathedral for an unforgettable behind-the-scenes trip. Matt goes on an underwater treasure hunt, and the whole family try out as choristers.

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun: Where Are They Now?
programme.desc: On the Costa del Sol, were back at Adam and Alicias busy cocktail bar. Coach tour operator Lesley trials new Spanish culinary experiences to get her business back on the road. And Colin and Kim have finished an impressive gîte renovation in France.

programme.title: Jasmine Harmans Renovation in the Sun
programme.desc: With the kitchen totally demolished and the new one arriving in just a week, panic starts to set in. The family leave the builders hard at work and visit a local dog rescue charity, and some finishing touches are put to the sun terraces.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Tina loves France and hopes to retire there. She has a budget of £160,000. Danni Menzies hunts around the beautiful Cote de Beziers in southern France with Tina and her daughter Clare.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jean Johansson heads to Mayenne in France to help engineer Martin and retired gardener Mandy find their dream forever home. She shows them five great properties for their budget of £50,000.

programme.title: Chateau DIY
programme.desc: Ben and Billie embark on a dramatic two-bedroom flat makeover for a Valentines Day event. Nick and Nicole battle to turn a crumbling façade into a striking showpiece.

programme.title: Kirstie and Phils Love It or List It: Brilliant Builds
programme.desc: A look back at one of Kirstie and Phils biggest builds, when one family went all in to overhaul their Huddersfield home beyond recognition. Paul and Dawns higgledy-piggledy layout didnt work for them anymore, so Kirstie got the sledgehammer out.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Boston, Lincolnshire the first host to kick things off is 23 year-old trainee accountant Annika. She might be young, but will her country fayre and love of game see her snag the cash prize?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Its the second night in Boston and host, competitions fanatic Katy, is convinced her organised approach will help her bag this weeks cash prize.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Its night three in Boston, and time for the only man in the competition to host. Adrian is hoping his spicy food and surprises will help him bag the prize, although it could be those surprises that end up costing him first place.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Its day four in Boston and Polish-born carer, Sylwia, is putting on a very European menu, hoping to charm with top notch cooking that takes her guests on a tour of all the places shes lived in the world.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Its the final night in Boston, and hard to please fox hunter, Carolyn, is planning on a charm offensive to win over the guests shes criticised all week.

programme.title: George Clarkes Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: George Clarke meets a master craftsman saving a Victorian circus wagon, and discovers a small hotel thats disguised as a tree and a camping pod that looks like a giant conker.

programme.title: The Great Celebrity Bake Off: Stand Up To Cancer
programme.desc: Emmett J Scanlan, Mark Wright, Nella Rose, Mutya Buena and Ralf Little make vegan fruit tarts and recreate their most ridiculous purchase ever.

programme.title: 24 Hours in Police Custody
programme.desc: The case of the brutal murder of 86-year-old Una Crown in 2013 is re-opened. Police think they know the killer but will the science back them up?

programme.title: 24 Hours in Police Custody
programme.desc: After forensics get a partial DNA match to a previous suspect, officers bring the man in for questioning. After 13 years of denial will he finally confess?

programme.desc: Britains sharpest armchair critics share more of their sharp, insightful, passionate and sometimes emotional critiques of the weeks biggest and best shows on television.

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: A hair stylist meets a nutritionist with a bit of swag. A conspiracy theorist is matched with a science teacher who also wrestles. And an opera singer serenades his date.

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: Married couple Abbie and Charlie are missing one thing from their family: a woman to turn them into a throuple. And glamour puss Chrissie is looking for husband number four.

programme.title: Open House: The Great Sex Experiment
programme.desc: Brendon and Priya want to explore Priyas bisexuality, but will her steamy hot tub and sensual time in the bedroom hit the spot? Chelsea and George disagree over how far they should take things.

programme.title: Open House: The Great Sex Experiment

programme.title: Cadbury vs McVities: Battle of the Brands
programme.desc: In the ultimate sweet-snack slugfest, from dunking to gifting to shrinking chocs, Kate Quilton discovers how these landmark brands are battling to be Britains top treat.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Dog handler Jon is forced to wrestle with a drunken man, Dan has an early-morning meeting with a drink-driver and a wine-loving shoplifter gets his just deserts. Meanwhile, Mark pulls over a juggernaut in jeopardy.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: A violent man gives the interceptors the runaround after wreaking havoc in a hospital, the driver of a stolen VW Beetle rewrites the highway code, a map-reading Macedonian truck driver has a bad day and a fuel thief is caught red-handed.

programme.title: Bite the Bullet
programme.desc: Epic story of a legend of the old West, the Endurance Horse Race, covering 700 miles of mountains and desert. Entrants include a hard-boiled ex-prostitute, a former cavalryman and an aristocratic Englishman looking for kicks.

programme.title: Mud Mountain Haulers
programme.desc: Craig and the crew are back in camp to log Saddle Ridge, but the crew discover the block is chock-full of boulders, making it impossible to log. One of the Glovers winch machines spews thick white smoke. They receive bad news from the government.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: Whether it be dealers moving supplies or users behind the wheel, it is a daily battle for the cops tackling drugs on Britains roads. Vehicle stops reveal drugs stashes worth up to £12,000.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Following the work of the Firearms Squad. The team respond to reports of a shotgun changing hands in the suburbs. When the team swoops in, both buyer and seller are taken by surprise.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: A high-octane episode kicks off with the interceptors in hot pursuit. The lads abandon their car and disappear into the woods, however, the dog unit is dispatched to sniff them out.

programme.title: Motorway Cops: Catching Britains Speeders
programme.desc: Humber Bridge staff alert police to a car has just passed through stinking of cannabis. And in Grimsby, a dog unit chases a car that fails to stop.

programme.title: Police: Night Shift 999
programme.desc: In Gloucestershire, the night shift police race to catch burglars with the help of police dog Elmo, and a helicopter joins the search for a driver who crashed into a gas main and disappears into the night.

programme.title: Cops
programme.desc: A business owner flags down officers when an erratic man starts climbing on his car; unusual circumstances allow officers to cut a driver some slack for being honest; a man forgets to return a car after running errands.

programme.title: Cops
programme.desc: A suspect on the run makes a dangerous decision when he finds himself cornered; an unsuspecting driver gets more than she bargained for when she gives an old friend a lift; a friendly woman has a thing for cowboys.

programme.title: The X-Files
programme.desc: When Mulder finds out that Scully was kidnapped by Duane Barry, he risks the wrath of his superiors and the annoyance of Agent Krycek in order to do whatever it takes to find her. Meanwhile, Krycek is found to be reporting Mulders activities to the CSM.

programme.title: Scam City
programme.desc: Tourists have become the spectators and the prize in the gritty underworld that crawls under the thriving tourist scene of Rome.

programme.title: Outback Truckers
programme.desc: Theres trouble in the wind for Kurt who is struggling with a load causing oversized problems. For Johnny on the Tanami Track, its too hard one minute and too soft the next.

programme.title: Trucking Hell
programme.desc: Jim and his team face a difficult foggy morning on the M1 when a crash between two artics causes a five-mile-long tailback.

programme.title: Trucking Hell
programme.desc: Veteran driver Jim attends an emergency call-out to recover a lorry that has broken down bang in the middle of a busy ring road. Motor racing fanatic Merrie has a hunch she may have landed her dream job when she attends a broken down lorry.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Lisa and Lewis tackle a troublesome lad with a potty mouth, followed by a trip to the canal, where a member of the public has heard gunshots. Phil Broughton is hot on the heels of a fail-to-stop Ford pushing speeds of over a ton.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: A van driver blocks himself into a dead end and repeatedly rams the interceptors car, a self-confident drink driver gets argumentative, and reports arrive of a wanted vehicle travelling towards the Nottinghamshire border.

programme.title: Mud Mountain Haulers
programme.desc: A loader malfunction causes a huge bottleneck on the landing. Tempers flare when Craig and Taylor attempt to relocate the 65-ton winch machine. The Glovers are moving to a new block on Erie Mountain, but a critical piece of machinery goes down.

programme.title: Mud Mountain Haulers
programme.desc: Craig and his crew log a burned section of forest north of Kamloops, but the lethal mix of ash and rainwater takes a toll on the machines and the crew. Brent and his crew clear a fire guard around a clients homestead to protect it from wildfires.

programme.title: Mud Mountain Haulers
programme.desc: The Glovers fight harsh winter conditions on the highway while low bedding their 45-ton winch machine to Erie Mountain. When winter slams Gollen, Craig steps up his exit strategy, but breakdowns and a collision on the block threaten to delay the job.

programme.desc: Mulder reluctantly accepts a new partner, Agent Alex Krycek, and they investigate the deaths of several Green Beret soldiers who participated in a sleep deprivation experiment during the Vietnam War.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With Rosss New Girlfriend

programme.title: Ooh the Banter: 100 Years of Scottish Comedy
programme.desc: Jack Docherty brings together a jam-packed cast of comedians, actors and famous faces for a riotous celebration of our most valuable export - our sense of humour. Scotland is a small nation with a big funny bone. Were known the world over for our self-deprecation, quick-witted patter and deadpan asides. But what makes us so funny? To find out the answer we delve deep into the BBC Scotland archives where we find a centurys worth of classic characters, catchphrases and comedy clips.

programme.title: Growing Up Scottish

programme.desc: Brazils favelas are considered extremely dangerous, making them one of the worlds most deadly places to grow up. As a result of poverty and a lack of government and police control, many young men here resort to drug dealing and gang crime under the control of the drug cartels. In other neighbourhoods, heavily armed criminal militia groups control day-to-day life with an iron grip, extorting residents - fail to pay or cross them, and the consequences can be deadly.

programme.desc: Dion has to navigate his way through an eight bedroom behemoth in Bangor. Jacqui takes in the views in Dover. And Tommy is in Lincolnshire to look around a bungalow.

programme.title: Just One Thing
programme.desc: Zoe Ball heads to south London to convince charity worker Latoya that gardening could be her secret wellbeing weapon. Shes a total beginner, but the science is firmly on her side. Getting hands on with soil can build strength, bolster the immune system and reduce stress. If Latoya is willing to get her hands dirty, she could see her health blossom.

programme.title: Ronan Keatings Wild Atlantic
programme.desc: Ronan heads to Irelands southernmost and largest county to meet up with family, compete in the ancient sport of road bowling and explore a Tibetan Buddhist temple on the coast.

programme.title: The Tournament

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons presents the electrifyingly fast quiz with a £3,000 jackpot. Six plucky contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions whilst our ruthless spotlight patrols the studio.

programme.title: Clive Myries Caribbean Adventure
programme.desc: In Cubas capital, Clive realises a boyhood dream, taking on a potential Olympic champion at one of Havanas legendary boxing gyms. Plus, he visits a car mechanic to get under the hood of a classic Chevy.

programme.title: Ancient Invisible Cities
programme.desc: Classical historian Dr Michael Scott explores the often-invisible treasures of one of the greatest ancient cities in the world: Istanbul. The city has been at the crossroads of Europe and Asia for over two and a half millennia. From the Greeks and Romans through the Ottomans to the Turks, Istanbul has been fought over, destroyed and rebuilt time after time.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Michael Aspel and a team of experts invite members of the public in Nottingham to bring along their antiques for examination. Discoveries of note include a pair of outsize boots, an important collection of memorabilia from the Nuremberg trials, and an Andy Pandy memento which is closely related to one of the experts. Plus, a shirt worn by an Arsenal player in the 1950 Cup tie and a valuable ring found in a teapot spout.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Members of the public are invited to try to make money out of their antiques.

programme.title: Richard Osmans House of Games
programme.desc: Every day this week, celebrity contestants Jacqui Joseph, Ben Thompson, Laura Tobin and Henning Wehn take on a series of quiz rounds selected by the host, quiz legend Richard Osman.

programme.title: Great Japanese Railway Journeys
programme.sub-title: Japanese Alps: Nagano to Narai Juku
programme.desc: Michael ends the first leg of his Japan adventure on a Shinkansen bullet train, heading to Nagano, the host city of the 1998 Winter Olympics and the gateway to the Japanese Alps.

programme.title: Greatest Gardens with Diarmuid Gavin and Carol Klein
programme.desc: Garden experts Diarmuid and Carol are joined by guest judge and passionate gardener Patrick Grant to continue the search to find Northern Irelands Greatest Garden.

programme.title: Beechgrove Garden
programme.desc: Calum kicks off his season at Beechgrove demonstrating what can be grown from just one container in one season, while Brian looks at how to keep lawns looking great.

programme.title: Gardeners World
programme.desc: Horticulturists offer various techniques to elevate the home gardening experience, from repositioning roses in rustic layouts to planning vibrant spring displays.

programme.title: Spotlight
programme.desc: The scam starts with a small repair, then escalates into major work that will supposedly save your home - but its really funding a lavish life for the scammers. Their chief weapon is Irish charm: the travelling conmen have been crossing America, convincing homeowners to part with millions of dollars. With the FBI on their case, Conor Spackman follows the money trail and lifts the lid on where the cash goes.

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Phil Wang introduces the talented musical comedian Rachel Parris and charismatic Brennan Reece to the stage.

programme.desc: Cult mystery thriller in which a police sergeant visits a remote Scottish isle to search for a missing girl but fears for her safety when he finds a community steeped in paganism. The community deny the girls existence and this, coupled with the sergeants Christian background, fuels his suspicions - and growing unease - as the investigation continues.

programme.title: Panorama

programme.title: Shift the Thrift
programme.desc: In High Wycombe, mother and daughter Liz and Alice battle to raise money for Great Ormond Street Hospital, gambling on vintage fashion, ceramics and a bold sewing machine buy.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion has to navigate his way through an eight bedroom behemoth in Bangor. Jacqui takes in the views in Dover. And Tommy is in Lincolnshire to look around a bungalow.

programme.title: Just One Thing
programme.desc: Zoe Ball heads to south London to convince charity worker Latoya that gardening could be her secret wellbeing weapon. Shes a total beginner, but the science is firmly on her side. Getting hands on with soil can build strength, bolster the immune system and reduce stress. If Latoya is willing to get her hands dirty, she could see her health blossom.

programme.title: Ronan Keatings Wild Atlantic
programme.desc: Ronan heads to Irelands southernmost and largest county to meet up with family, compete in the ancient sport of road bowling and explore a Tibetan Buddhist temple on the coast.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Thirty players compete across the series, scoring points by answering questions correctly. However, they must avoid the impossible answers, otherwise they will be eliminated from the show until the next day.

programme.title: The Tournament
programme.desc: Eight contestants compete in a series of fast-paced quiz battles in an attempt to eliminate their opponents from the days game and walk away with a cash prize.

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons presents the electrifyingly fast quiz with a £3,000 jackpot. Six plucky contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions whilst our ruthless spotlight patrols the studio.

programme.title: Clive Myries Caribbean Adventure
programme.desc: In Cubas capital, Clive realises a boyhood dream, taking on a potential Olympic champion at one of Havanas legendary boxing gyms. Plus, he visits a car mechanic to get under the hood of a classic Chevy.

programme.title: Ancient Invisible Cities
programme.desc: Classical historian Dr Michael Scott explores the often-invisible treasures of one of the greatest ancient cities in the world: Istanbul. The city has been at the crossroads of Europe and Asia for over two and a half millennia. From the Greeks and Romans through the Ottomans to the Turks, Istanbul has been fought over, destroyed and rebuilt time after time.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Michael Aspel and a team of experts invite members of the public in Nottingham to bring along their antiques for examination. Discoveries of note include a pair of outsize boots, an important collection of memorabilia from the Nuremberg trials, and an Andy Pandy memento which is closely related to one of the experts. Plus, a shirt worn by an Arsenal player in the 1950 Cup tie and a valuable ring found in a teapot spout.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Members of the public are invited to try to make money out of their antiques.

programme.title: Richard Osmans House of Games
programme.desc: Every day this week, celebrity contestants Jacqui Joseph, Ben Thompson, Laura Tobin and Henning Wehn take on a series of quiz rounds selected by the host, quiz legend Richard Osman.

programme.title: Great Japanese Railway Journeys
programme.sub-title: Japanese Alps: Nagano to Narai Juku
programme.desc: Michael ends the first leg of his Japan adventure on a Shinkansen bullet train, heading to Nagano, the host city of the 1998 Winter Olympics and the gateway to the Japanese Alps.

programme.title: Greatest Gardens with Diarmuid Gavin and Carol Klein
programme.desc: Garden experts Diarmuid and Carol are joined by guest judge and passionate gardener Patrick Grant to continue the search to find Northern Irelands Greatest Garden.

programme.title: Beechgrove Garden
programme.desc: Calum kicks off his season at Beechgrove demonstrating what can be grown from just one container in one season, while Brian looks at how to keep lawns looking great.

programme.title: Gardeners World
programme.desc: Horticulturists offer various techniques to elevate the home gardening experience, from repositioning roses in rustic layouts to planning vibrant spring displays.

programme.title: Spotlight
programme.desc: The scam starts with a small repair, then escalates into major work that will supposedly save your home - but its really funding a lavish life for the scammers. Their chief weapon is Irish charm: the travelling conmen have been crossing America, convincing homeowners to part with millions of dollars. With the FBI on their case, Conor Spackman follows the money trail and lifts the lid on where the cash goes.

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Phil Wang introduces the talented musical comedian Rachel Parris and charismatic Brennan Reece to the stage.

programme.title: Newsnight

programme.title: The Wicker Man
programme.desc: Cult mystery thriller in which a police sergeant visits a remote Scottish isle to search for a missing girl but fears for her safety when he finds a community steeped in paganism. The community deny the girls existence and this, coupled with the sergeants Christian background, fuels his suspicions - and growing unease - as the investigation continues.

Before long, the stack of toilet rolls is so high it nearly reaches the ceiling! Back on the Big Red Boat, the Twirlywoos get a visit from the Very Important Lady.

programme.title: Something Special

The Mini Monsters create paper cup critter puppets, and Rebeccas cousin, Fiona, calls to share her idea for a cupcake case flower. Murphis mega monster make is a teddy bear box, helped by some round boxes brought along by the Recycling Express.

programme.title: Vegesaurs
programme.desc: Ginger and the Peas invite Kid Brocc to a sleepover, but can he adjust to a new place?

programme.title: Bluey

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Duggee is wearing his new scent which he has made himself. The Squirrels want to make their own perfumes too. First, they must find their favourite smells so they can extract their essence, then mix them together.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Duggee is a big, friendly dog who runs The Squirrel Club, an after-school activity club where children can learn and take part in all kinds of activities, have fun adventures, and earn badges for their achievements.

programme.title: Lets Go for a Walk
programme.desc: Ranger Hamza and the Ramblers walk along the river to the city farm. On the way, they visit a pigeon hotel, count the floors on a building, see some sheep and even pretend to be trolls.

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: Amanpreet challenges her sister Amrit to a biking competition to see who can cycle around the bike track the fastest. The bikes get really muddy, so Amanpreet takes charge of giving them a good clean and has some splashy fun.

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Today it is the turn of talented young cook Mason, who is making a traditional recipe from Yorkshire. Masons recipe, yorkshire pudding, is named after the place it comes from, and he hopes all his friends will like it as much as he does. Mason serves his recipe with broccoli, parsley and traditional Yorkshire gravy. One of his ingredients is horseradish, which none of his friends like the taste of at first.

programme.title: Do You Know?
programme.desc: Maddie is finding out how wearing glasses help to see more clearly. She visits an optician to see how an eye test works by having one herself. She reads the charts and shapes on the wall and looks into a special camera.

programme.title: Dog Squad
programme.desc: Meagaidh the Border Collie is a search and rescue dog who works with her owner, Tom. Her supernose follows scents and smells like clues, so they can rescue people who need their help. Annie is in a kayak, but shes lost her map and is totally lost. Shes managed to paddle to a beach, but shes cold and wet and has hurt her wrist. At the Woofton Search and Rescue Centre, Tom and Meagaidh need to find Annie, and fast!

programme.title: Steve and Aneeshwar Go Wild
programme.desc: Steve and Aneeshwar give Winnie the white rhino a mud bath. They find out how the mud keeps its skin nice and healthy. Their next encounter is with hissing cockroaches, which they get to hold so that they can get a closer look at this unique animal.

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: AJ takes Evie and Anaya swimming, but Waffle must stay at home with Simon. Refusing to miss out on the fun, Waffle begins an adventure of his own and sets off to find Evie.

programme.desc: The puffin family enjoy a night-time adventure with a friendly frog.

programme.title: Yukee

programme.title: Hey Duggee

programme.desc: Ranger Hamza and the Ramblers discover how seaweed plays such an important role in the environment. They find out that seaweed can be a shelter for lots of sea creatures, a great place for seabirds to look for food to eat, and that seaweed helps provide some of the oxygen we breathe. On the way, they see what they can spot in a rock pool and have a picnic of some snacks made from seaweed!

programme.title: Deal or No Deal
programme.desc: Airing from 2005 to 2009 on NBC, the game show series returns to primetime TV with original host Howie Mandel. A contestant must choose from 26 sealed briefcases containing a marker for various amounts of cash -- from one penny to $1 million. The player then eliminates the remaining 25 cases one by one. The chosen ones are opened and the amount of money inside revealed. After several cases are opened, the player is tempted by the Banker to accept an offer of cash in exchange for not continuing the game and possibly winning a larger sum of money.

After several cases are opened, the player is tempted by the Banker to accept an offer of cash in exchange for not continuing the game and possibly winning a larger sum of money.

programme.title: Gold Rush
programme.desc: The boys are back in Alaska (although Discovery dropped the states mention in the title after the first season) hoping to strike it rich mining for gold. Their first effort was anything but rich and glorious, however, as the crew led by Todd Hoffman and his father, Jack, sunk $250,000 into their dream and came away with just $20,000 worth of gold. The dismal summer was filled with injuries, malfunctioning equipment and constant fighting among the greenhorn miners, yet their serious case of gold fever trumps any talk of giving up and leads to a new operating plan in order to salvage their dream. The series also keeps tabs on the efforts of teenager Parker Schnabel and old-timer Dakota Fred to hit the mother lode at separate sites.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats
programme.desc: With Sarah Millican, Micky Flanagan, Georgie Thompson and Louie Spence.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats
programme.desc: The first of a two-part best bits compilation sees Jimmy Carr and team captains Sean Lock and Jon Richardson dive into the world of polls, surveys and statistics with a bevvy of celebrity guests from the current series.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Cam embarks on a juice fast and Mitch is helpless as he watches him go through the different stages of dieting. Plus, Phil decides to take up the tightrope.

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: When Barry and Joanne crash Erica and Geoffs peaceful weekend camping trip, they end up in a competition to see who is the better couple; Adam seeks to prove to Brea that he doesnt have an easy, privileged life.

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Beverly involves herself in Erica and Barrys relationships; Adam and Brea are cast opposite of each other in the school play, but their excitement for one anothers success grows dim when one starts to outshine the other onstage.

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Adrenaline junkie Mark wants to fly high and take to the sky on his big day, but his straight-talking bride-to-be Lynsey wants more personal, no-nonsense nuptials.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Mitchell and Cam settle into their new normal and Phil and Claire decide that one of the kids needs to move out. As Gloria becomes more successful at work, she notices Jay and the kids dont seem to need her as much.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Phil and Claire are annoyed at the chaos at home and decide that one of the kids have to move out. Cam gets a call that offers him his dream job and Jay has a special surprise for Gloria.

programme.title: Rude(ish) Tube
programme.desc: A short clip from Rude Tubes naughty little sister Rude-ish Tube, featuring food and animals.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay revisits Cafe Hon in Hampden, Maryland, Chiarellas in Philadelphia, and Leones in Montclair, New Jersey, to see whether the restaurant owners have taken his advice on board.

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Erica goes on a date with a guy from the coffee shop, while Geoff ends up on an episode of "The Dating Game"; Beverly is thrilled to learn that Murray has spontaneously purchased a shore house.

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Following Erica and Geoffs breakup, Murray decides to take Erica out to cheer her up; Adam is excited to participate in the school senior prank until his conscience catches up with him and he looks to Beverly to help get him out of it.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Mitch and Cam have a big decision to make, one that involves a spot of sleuthing and the parenting skills of certain family members.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Claire and Glorias plan to spend quality time with their kids in the outdoors backfires. Plus, Phil witnesses an unexpected moment of weakness from Jay.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.sub-title: Im Just a Girl Who Cant Say Doh
programme.desc: Marge puts on Lisas play when she bags a big job at the theatre. Homer goes gaga over Maggies baby class supervisor.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: The Simpsons holiday at Niagara Falls, where Marge must rescue Lisa when a mix-up sees her mistakenly granted asylum in Canada. Elsewhere, Homer takes Maggie to a `Daddy and Me class.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: After Homers work cuts childrens health care benefits, Marge turns to buying healing crystals as a cheaper solution for Barts ADD. Bart begins to succeed in school, prompting Marge to open her own crystals empire.

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: An English teacher with 25 facial piercings meets a bibliophile whos never had a boyfriend. And a 70-year-old joker has a date with a dance-loving 69-year-old whos into grinding.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon head to the historic Olde Hitching Post in Hanson, Massachusetts, where he meets an owner so stubborn and set in his ways that he doesnt realise hes the reason his restaurant is losing money. Owner Tom bought the business for his daughter Andrea, but six years later he is still refusing to let her run it. Gordon must fight a controlling boss who doesnt react well to his criticism.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Lisa becomes the anchor of a kids news programme and is irritated when Bart is made co-anchor.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart goes from boyhood to adulthood in a coming-of-age story chronicling his life from six years old to a fully accomplished young man.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: While Phil spends some quality time with Haley, taking her on a college tour of his old alma mater, Gloria and Jay are forced to have `the talk with Manny.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: When a neighbours house burns down, the gang immediately roll up their sleeves to help out. Even their best-intentioned plans dont run smoothly though.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.sub-title: How Lisa Got Her Marge Back

programme.title: The Simpsons

programme.desc: Jessica, whos 28, is a real-life Bridget Jones who wants the perfect man to bring out her naughty side. Opera singer Clare, whos 21, seeks someone who hits all the right notes.

programme.title: Naked Attraction

programme.title: Let Him Go
programme.desc: Following the loss of their son, a retired sheriff and his wife leave their Montana ranch to rescue their young grandson from the clutches of a dangerous family living off the grid in the Dakotas.

programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Jubal
programme.desc: Western version of `Othello in which a down-on-his-luck drifter takes a job at a ranch where the bosss sensuous wife takes a shine to him. Trouble is in store when the womans ex-lover feels slighted and determines to cause trouble for the newcomer.

programme.title: Anzio
programme.desc: American troops land unopposed on Italian beaches during World War II, but instead of pushing on to Rome, they dig in and the Germans fight back ferociously.

programme.title: Broken Arrow
programme.desc: Set in Arizona in 1870, gold prospector Tom Jeffords is tired of the repetitive reprisal killings between settlers and the indigenous locals and plans a visit to the Apache leader, Cochise, to try and arrange a truce. After first being welcomed into the fold by Cochise, and even marrying the chiefs daughter, old racial problems resurface.

programme.title: Son of Robin Hood
programme.desc: The merry men find themselves a man to lead them once they learn Robin Hoods son is actually a daughter.

programme.title: The Eagle
programme.desc: Accompanied by his slave, a Roman centurion heads to Scotlands Highlands to retrieve the long-lost Ninth Legions golden eagle standard and restore his fathers honour.

programme.title: Knock at the Cabin
programme.desc: While holidaying at a remote cabin, a young girl and her parents are taken hostage by four armed strangers, demanding that the family make an unthinkable choice to avert the apocalypse.

programme.title: Mission: Impossible -- Ghost Protocol
programme.desc: After the IMF are implicated in the bombing of the Kremlin, Ethan Hunt and his new team go rogue. In order to clear their organisations name, they track down a dangerous terrorist named Kurt Hendricks. Hendricks has gained access to Russian nuclear launch codes and is planning a strike on the United States, but will the new team be able to stop the impending disaster?

programme.title: First Love
programme.desc: In Tokyo, a young down on his luck boxer meets his first love, not realising that she is caught up in a drug-smuggling scheme.

programme.title: James Martins Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James takes a magical trip to the stunning Isle of Skye, where hes joined by top chef and ultimate Scotsman Tony Singh. Tony visits a local bakery which doubles as an art gallery and learns to make the local oatcakes. James is inspired by the amazing local seafood and cooks up a tasty dish using plaice and lovage. Their day comes to a perfect end aboard a boat, where they spot dozens of dolphins, and James cooks up a fresh lobster and langoustine dish with an added Scottish twist.

programme.title: Rick Steins Food Heroes: Another Helping
programme.desc: In the Clovelly area of North Devon, Rick goes fishing for herring with Steve Perham, whose family have been fishermen for over 200 years. All that fresh sea air gives them a hearty appetite as Rick fries the herring in wholemeal flour.

programme.title: Rick Steins Food Heroes: Another Helping
programme.desc: Rick and Chalky travel to Loch Torridon in Scotland to go fishing with John McGreggor for langoustines. Here the prawns are protected by the Marien Stewardship Council, which has awarded its seal of approval to the fishermen.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree Drummond is filling her freezer with breakfast favourites and feeding the guys out working cattle. Her to-make list includes freezer breakfast paninis, very berry croissants and frozen yoghurt bark.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree Drummond improves her potluck game with four portable ideas for barbecue season. She serves up an eight-layer dip, barbecue potato salad, blueberry cornbread panzanella, and a lemon icebox pie.

programme.title: Jamies Money Saving Meals
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver makes a frugal but amazing paella with chicken thighs, spicy chorizo and prawns from the freezer. Theres also crisp Cajun fishcakes with a classic chopped salad and Jamies snake in the hole.

programme.title: Jamies Money Saving Meals
programme.desc: Jamie makes a thrifty curry with chicken drumsticks and frugal but posh beans on toast.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.sub-title: Cooking for Jeffrey: Back to School Surprise
programme.desc: Jeffrey is going back to school after a long holiday, so Ina Garten prepares an alfresco dinner of Moroccan grilled lamb chops, couscous with pine nuts and mint, and roasted vine tomatoes.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: Ina is teaching Dinner Party 101 to her great friend, actress Debra Monk. The foolproof meal plan includes roast chicken with bread and rocket salad, a stunning easy cheese board, tri-berry crumbles and a table-setting tutorial.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: `The Great British Bake Off makes its debut on Channel 4 as the series returns for an eighth season with 12 budding bakers competing to be crowned the champion. Established judge Paul Hollywood is joined by food connoisseur Prue Leith, while seasoned entertainers Sandi Toksvig and Noel Fielding take on the role of being the shows hosts. Despite the change in personnel, the key Bake Off traditions remain intact, as the contestants try to impress the panel with a fine selection of beautifully-crafted and delicious cakes, tarts, breads and more.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: `The Great British Bake Off makes its debut on Channel 4 as the series returns for an eighth season with 12 budding bakers competing to be crowned the champion. Established judge Paul Hollywood is joined by food connoisseur Prue Leith, while seasoned entertainers Sandi Toksvig and Noel Fielding take on the role of being the shows hosts. Despite the change in personnel, the key Bake Off traditions remain intact, as the contestants try to impress the panel with a fine selection of beautifully-crafted and delicious cakes, tarts, breads and more.

programme.title: James Martin Delicious
programme.desc: Cef James Martin produces a dazzling array of recipes using staples such as marinated vegetables, cold meats, cheeses and fresh pasta. Here, James cooks rhubarb and ginger crumble, basil and soft fruit terrine and plum chutney. He also visits a local grower of organic rhubarb.

programme.title: James Martin Delicious
programme.desc: Its a mozzarella mouthful as James prepares aubergine and mozzarella stacks, marinated mozzarella, and tomato and mozzarella tart, and visits a farm on the Hampshire Downs that has a herd of buffalo, would you believe?

programme.title: James Martin Delicious
programme.desc: James Martin prepares chicken with flageolet beans and fennel, sea bass with summer herbs and basil oil tuna, and discusses different types of olives with his regular supplier.

programme.title: James Martin Delicious
programme.desc: James Martin uses honey to create dishes such as cod with honey onions, apple tart with honey and walnut ice cream and saffron and honey pears, then he dons a bee suit to enter a hive.

programme.title: Rick Steins Food Heroes: Another Helping
programme.desc: In the Clovelly area of North Devon, Rick goes fishing for herring with Steve Perham, whose family have been fishermen for over 200 years. All that fresh sea air gives them a hearty appetite as Rick fries the herring in wholemeal flour.

programme.title: Rick Steins Food Heroes: Another Helping
programme.desc: Rick and Chalky travel to Loch Torridon in Scotland to go fishing with John McGreggor for langoustines. Here the prawns are protected by the Marien Stewardship Council, which has awarded its seal of approval to the fishermen.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree Drummond is filling her freezer with breakfast favourites and feeding the guys out working cattle. Her to-make list includes freezer breakfast paninis, very berry croissants and frozen yoghurt bark.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree Drummond improves her potluck game with four portable ideas for barbecue season. She serves up an eight-layer dip, barbecue potato salad, blueberry cornbread panzanella, and a lemon icebox pie.

programme.title: Jamies Money Saving Meals
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver makes a frugal but amazing paella with chicken thighs, spicy chorizo and prawns from the freezer. Theres also crisp Cajun fishcakes with a classic chopped salad and Jamies snake in the hole.

programme.title: Jamies Money Saving Meals
programme.desc: Jamie makes a thrifty curry with chicken drumsticks and frugal but posh beans on toast.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.sub-title: Cooking for Jeffrey: Back to School Surprise
programme.desc: Jeffrey is going back to school after a long holiday, so Ina Garten prepares an alfresco dinner of Moroccan grilled lamb chops, couscous with pine nuts and mint, and roasted vine tomatoes.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: Ina is teaching Dinner Party 101 to her great friend, actress Debra Monk. The foolproof meal plan includes roast chicken with bread and rocket salad, a stunning easy cheese board, tri-berry crumbles and a table-setting tutorial.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: `The Great British Bake Off makes its debut on Channel 4 as the series returns for an eighth season with 12 budding bakers competing to be crowned the champion. Established judge Paul Hollywood is joined by food connoisseur Prue Leith, while seasoned entertainers Sandi Toksvig and Noel Fielding take on the role of being the shows hosts. Despite the change in personnel, the key Bake Off traditions remain intact, as the contestants try to impress the panel with a fine selection of beautifully-crafted and delicious cakes, tarts, breads and more.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: `The Great British Bake Off makes its debut on Channel 4 as the series returns for an eighth season with 12 budding bakers competing to be crowned the champion. Established judge Paul Hollywood is joined by food connoisseur Prue Leith, while seasoned entertainers Sandi Toksvig and Noel Fielding take on the role of being the shows hosts. Despite the change in personnel, the key Bake Off traditions remain intact, as the contestants try to impress the panel with a fine selection of beautifully-crafted and delicious cakes, tarts, breads and more.

programme.title: Ottolenghis Mediterranean Feast
programme.desc: Yotam Ottolenghis journey continues in Turkeys most historical and cultural city, Istanbul. Here, he tastes fish from the magnificent waters of the Bosphorus, is amazed by beautiful local vegetables and samples mouth-watering street food. He begins by making a Turkish pizza topped with spicy beef, pine nuts and a crunchy salad, baked in one of Istanbuls largest traditional stone ovens.

programme.title: Adam Richman Eats Italy
programme.desc: Foodie Adam Richman embarks on a delicious culinary adventure across Italy. He visits the places that are home to the most iconic Italian dishes.

programme.title: Adam Richman Eats Italy
programme.desc: Foodie Adam Richman embarks on a delicious culinary adventure across Italy. He visits the places that are home to the most iconic Italian dishes.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: This trip, Guys getting up close to some serious gourmet grub. In Chicago, the brewhouse is serving up beer to go with mussels, a mega brisket sandwich and their version of head cheese.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri digs into the traditions of many different cultures including a Mexican joint in Houston and a Jewish deli-on-wheels in Orlando, Florida.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: In Virginia Beach, a real deal barbeque joint serves up burnt ends on a bun with homemade rub and smokey sauce. In San Antonio, Texas, a Greek owner hand rolls tortillas for Mexican favourites.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Mofongo, homemade focaccia and stuffed meatballs.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Casey Webb journeys across America to tackle daunting food challenges and sample iconic regional dishes. Enormous food options create problems for even the most voracious of eaters.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Casey Webb journeys across America to tackle daunting food challenges and sample iconic regional dishes. Enormous food options create problems for even the most voracious of eaters.

programme.title: Cake Boss
programme.desc: Buddy Valastro and his cake crew are back and whipping up amazing cake creations. They prove that no matter how sticky the situation, the family that bakes together stays together.

programme.title: Cake Boss
programme.desc: Buddy receives a special order: his long-time employee, Nikki, is getting married. But being around cakes for years doesnt mean Nikki knows what she wants.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: This trip, some of Guys favourites are totally homemade from coast to coast. In Manhattan, New York, a real deal chef is cranking out homemade pretzels with a special beer cheese sauce. In Vancouver, British Columbia, the butcher shop turned bakery and diner is serving up standards like burgers and turkey pot pie. And in Santa Barbara, California, the family-run joint scratch making authentic Mexican food.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: On this trip, Guys stepping into some family kitchens. In Vancouver, British Columbia, is a Chinese joint hand-pulling noodles and using age-old family recipes for dishes like beef rolls.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: This trip, Guys cruisin down the path less traveled. In Dallas, Guy turns tex-mex into mex-tex, with savory soups. In Baltimore, local rock star baker Duff Goldman stops by to seek out the secrets of the square pizza. In Santa Barbara, CA, a restaurant turnin turkey into fritters and a business into a family affair.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: This trip Guys rolling out for something new from the grill - and more. In San Antonio, Texas, a 60-year-old drive-in where the owners are putting a Thai twist on classic Texas bar-b-que.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: Ina is cooking up a fantastic meal with her friend Antonia. She makes gazpacho with goats cheese croutons, mustard marinated flank steak and Sicilian grilled swordfish.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: Ina Gartens sharing pot luck party secrets with local farmers Katie and Amanda, who are throwing a start of season bash.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: Inas on a cheese adventure and is sharing her favourite cheese recipes. She makes a show stopping spinach and cheddar souffle.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: Today is all about desserts in Inas hall of fame. She makes an incredible frozen mocha mousse, easy French apple galettes and balsamic strawberries.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.sub-title: Cooking for Jeffrey: Jeffreys Birthday Dinner
programme.desc: Ina Garten throws an Italian-themed birthday dinner with a big surprise for Jeffrey. To get the party started, theres bruschetta with sauteed chard, followed by roasted Italian meatballs, and a rocket and radicchio salad.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.sub-title: Cooking for Jeffrey: Deans for Dinner
programme.desc: Ina throws a celebratory meal for her friend Deborah Berke, who has been named the Dean of the Yale Architecture School. She makes a paella salad, goat cheese platter, English oat crackers and a pitcher of rose sangria.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver: Cooking for Less
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver makes a giant Yorkshire pudding with stuffing and roast veg, homemade gnocchi in a cheesy bacon and pea sauce, spicy kipper kedgeree and easy ice cream, all for under a quid a portion.

programme.title: Jamies Money Saving Meals
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver aims to inspire people to eat better and spend less on their weekly food bills. In this first episode - in which all recipes cost under £1.80 per portion - Jamie cooks up a glorious `mothership roast brisket of beef with all the trimmings. He then turns his leftover beef into an incredible Korean stir-fried rice.

programme.title: Jamies Money Saving Meals
programme.desc: Jamie cooks up a glorious roast half shoulder of pork with all the trimmings, and then turns his leftovers into stunning little dim sum buns. He then knocks up meatloaf with spaghetti, with a great tomato and chilli sauce.

programme.title: James Martins Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James is joined by Michelin-starred chef Kenny Atkinson, and they set out to sample the delights of Northumberland. They learn how mead is made on Holy Island, before James cooks up a Singapore-inspired crab and lobster dish on a stunning beach, and they both enjoy a Puffin cruise at Cockett Island. Theres a trip to a lobster hatchery too, before James cooks local speciality Singing Hinnies on another breathtaking beach.

programme.title: James Martins Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James is joined by Michelin-starred chef Lisa Goodwin-Allen for a visit to the Isle of Man during the famous TT race warm-up week. James serves up proper biker food, pork burgers with a spicy apple sauce, while Lisa visits local organic juice producers. James picks up some local Manx kippers and makes a delicious soup, before meeting TT legend John McGuinness. Finally, he treats John and his team to some tasty lamb chops with hispi cabbage and an olive glaze.

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Go Local
programme.desc: Bobby and Minal have built up a loyal customer base, winning a host of awards along the way, including a prestigious Michelin Bib Gourmand. But now they have their eyes on an even bigger prize, a coveted Michelin star.

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Go Local
programme.desc: Dave and Si are headed to The Stackpole Inn, an award-winning gastro pub in Pembrokeshire. Owners Gary and Becky first took it on 15 years ago. The couple were joined in 2019 by head chef Matt Waldron, who prides himself on using local ingredients.

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Go Local
programme.desc: The Bikers are in beautiful Northumberland, heading towards Wall, a small village just north of Hexham. Its an area with a bloody past of marauding Vikings and historic battles.

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Go West
programme.desc: Dave and Si are starting off their great adventure in Scotland. It was on these Scottish roads that they first became best friends, and theyre now returning to one of the places that holds very special memories for them both, the Isle of Bute.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver: Cooking for Less
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver makes a giant Yorkshire pudding with stuffing and roast veg, homemade gnocchi in a cheesy bacon and pea sauce, spicy kipper kedgeree and easy ice cream, all for under a quid a portion.

programme.title: Jamies Money Saving Meals
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver aims to inspire people to eat better and spend less on their weekly food bills. He then turns his leftover beef into an incredible Korean stir-fried rice.

programme.title: Jamies Money Saving Meals
programme.desc: Jamie cooks up a glorious roast half shoulder of pork with all the trimmings, and then turns his leftovers into stunning little dim sum buns. He then knocks up meatloaf with spaghetti, with a great tomato and chilli sauce.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guys traveling all over the country searching for a taste of the South. In Vancouver, British Columbia, Southern staples are served with a twist, like the pulled pork pancakes with a side of Jack Daniels syrup. In Charleston, South Carolina, the local football legend is cranking out some real deal comfort food with favourites like sweet tea-marinated pork chops and deep-fried hot dogs topped with chili.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: In Dundalk, Maryland, a Greek diner is decked out in neon and serving up family recipes. In Oxnard, California, a joint is baking, brewing, and cooking all in the same restaurant.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: On this trip, Guys teaming up with local legends. Outside Baltimore, Guy and Super Bowl champ Joe Theismann head to the kitchen for all types of tacos.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.sub-title: You Can Only Get It Here
programme.desc: This trip Guys finding funky flavours all over the country. In San Francisco, an Asian/American/Mexican fusion joint doing a Korean spicy pork burrito with homemade kimchee.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: This trip, Guys tasting his way through the day. In Tampa Bay, Florida, authentic Mexican street food for the lunch crowd is served out of bus, crankin out favourites like butternut squash tostadas. In Virginia Beach, Virginia, dinner at this popular seafood joint includes totally fresh oysters Rockefeller. And in San Antonio, Texas, a breakfast joint servin up waffles filled with bacon.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: This trip, in Detroit, Michigan, a pizza joint makes pizza pies with homemade toppings, and a unique crepe-filled lasagna. In Virginia, a beachside breakfast favourite the locals come running to for their citrus orange glazed pancakes and innovative benedicts. And in Chicago, Illinois, the tiny dive servin up flavour in massive proportions, like the melt-in-your-mouth pork belly sandwich.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the climax of the competition as the winning chefs cook at the Comic Relief Banquet at the Royal Albert Hall. Special guest and co-founder of the charity Richard Curtis welcomes guests - the unsung heroes of charity work, as well as those who have benefited from their help and some of the celebrities who have inspired us to do something funny for money.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three top chefs from Scotland are out to impress this weeks veteran judge and be crowned Scottish champion. Adam Handling has already won a number of industry awards and is full of confidence. His starter sets out to take the diner on a tour of the UKs best producers. Hes up against Michael Bremner, who is hoping to impress with contemporary ingredients including Birch sap and Douglas fir oil. Ally McGrath is celebrating his nations finest produce and making his very own version of haggis.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three newcomers to the competition create their fish dishes in a bid to be crowned Scottish champion. The standard is so high the veteran judge is moved to tears.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Ally McGrath has been keeping the veteran judge entertained with his very individual approach. Also, the highly competitive Adam Handling has been showcasing the finest produce grown and caught in and around the UK in his menu. Once again Michael Bremner is out to show how inventive he can be.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: One chef will go home and today the veteran judge finds himself with a difficult decision to make. Inventive Chef Michael Bremner is recreating the Scottish deserts of his childhood, Adam Handling is sticking with his trademark Asian flavours and Ally McGrath is tempering chocolate for the very first time.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Scotlands two highest scoring chefs are cooking for judges for the very first time. Both chefs have decided to make changes to some of their dishes, taking on board criticisms made during the week by the veteran judge.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Chef Josh Eggleton is back for a third time. Last year he made the national finals when his starter was shortlisted for the banquet, this year he intends to go one step further and cook at the banquet. One of his rivals last time, Jude Kereama is also back and out to do better than he did previously. The third competitor is newcomer Chris Wheeler who is originally from Swanage in Dorset.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: There are some exciting dishes on the menu as the three chefs from the South West tackle the fish course. Chef Josh Eggleton is hoping to impress with an original take on Bolognese, just one of six elements on his complex fish dish.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the main course and Chef Chris Wheeler is out to show just how theatrical he can be, his ideas are surprising everyone in the kitchen. No one knows quite what to expect and the veteran chef is hoping it tastes as good as it looks.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the dessert course and it is Josh Eggleton who has come up with the most ambitious dessert. He is creating his own self-serve ice cream made from vegetables, he is making everything from scratch including toppings and syrups.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the head to head and the South West regions two highest scoring chefs are cooking for judges Oliver Peyton, Prue Leith and Matthew Fort. They are joined by guest judge, journalist and restaurant critic, Grace Dent.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Two newcomers to the competition are challenging reigning North West champion Matt Worswick. Adam Reid is Head Chef for one of the UKs leading culinary talents, and Kim Woodward who made history when she was appointed Head Chef at the Savoy Grill.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the fish course and reigning North West champion Matt Worswick has reason to be confident. However, Kims confident she has got one up on Matt by baking her own black bread unlike Matt whos taking a shortcut.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the main course of the North West Regional heat and the scores mean this is a crucial round. Matt Worswick may be the reigning champion but he is not yet assured of a place cooking for the judges and he is feeling the strain.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the dessert course and the three North West competitors are getting creative, producing some of the most original and stunning desserts. All three chefs are working in a hot kitchen, the tension is palpable, and this time one chef will go home.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two chefs are cooking for judges Matthew Fort, Prue Leith and Oliver Peyton, and guest judge Lady Claire MacDonald OBE. There are stand out dishes from both chefs making it a difficult contest to judge.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three of the finest chefs from Wales are competing to be crowned champion. Two chefs are back for a second time: Phil Carmichael, from Cowbridge, South Wales, is Executive Chef at Berners Tavern in London and Adam Bannister, is chef and co-owner of Slice in his home city of Swansea. Adam was crowed Welsh champion last year so this year Phil is back with something to prove. He plans to go home raising the flag for Wales. The third chef is newcomer Andrew Birch, also from Swansea.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the fish course and newcomer Andrew Birch is confident, he is passionate about seafood. He is celebrating the cockle which is harvested near his home city of Swansea. Adam Bannister has created an alter ego, Captain Bannister for his modern take on the old boil in the bag fish dinners and Phil has chosen to revamp the classic prawn cocktail.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Phil Carmichael is serving up his version of an OBE, a refined take on onions, bacon and egg. Adam is planning to serve up Wild boar on a spit, while Andrew turns up the heat when he combines strong Japanese flavours with a traditional roast.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the dessert course and one chef will go home. Phil Carmichael is determined it wont be him but not everything is going to plan when he tackles his Cup of Tea for Maam. One of the accompaniments to his earl grey panna cotta just wont work. He has to make a difficult decision.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the head to head. The two highest scoring chefs from Wales are cooking for judges Oliver Peyton, Prue Leith and Matthew Fort who are joined by guest judge, blogger and food writer Rosie Birkett.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three newcomers are competing to become regional champion. Daniel Smith, a former head chef for the Roux brothers, now runs his own restaurant in his beloved home county of Norfolk. Andrew Scotts worked with the best in the business, and hes concentrating on telling powerful stories as well as delivering perfect flavours. Danny Gill is a former head chef at Daniel Cliffords two star restaurant Midsummer House.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the fish course and the chefs are using some of the UKs best seafood: lobster, salmon and Cornish crab. Danny Gill is a chef who likes to use the very latest equipment to create the most intense flavours. Hes going to great lengths to maximise the taste of his cucumber consomme and it is causing a lot of interest in the kitchen.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Two chefs are cooking venison. Danny Gill is making a venison wellington, inspired by his Dad, a former RAF chef who has served up Wellington for royalty at many air force banquets. While Andrew Scott has sourced his venison from the Queens Scottish estate, Balmoral.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: This week three chefs from the central region are fighting to be crowned champion. One competitor will go home, only the two highest scoring chefs will make it through to the judging chamber. Danny Gill is facing his toughest day yet, choosing to make a staggering eight elements for his imaginative dessert.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The Central regions two highest scoring chefs are cooking for judges Oliver Peyton, Prue Leith and Matthew Fort, for the very first time. Joining them in the chamber is guest judge Enam Ali MBE, a food entrepreneur who set up The British Curry Awards.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three of the most exciting talents from London and the South East region are competing for a place in the National Finals. Head chef Mark Froydenlund runs Marcus Wareings two star restaurant. Russell Bateman is head Chef at The Grove in Hertfordshire and Ronnie Murray who heads up the Hix Restaurant group.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the fish course and chef Russell Bateman is celebrating the diversity of his beloved home town of London - by combining lobster with jerk spices for an ambitious fish course with seven key elements. Ronnie Murrays taking a risk with an ingredient which is close to the veteran judges heart...and is planning to serve it at three different temperatures warm, deep fried and frozen. And two Michelin starred Mark Froydenlund is planning a novel way to present his dish.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the all-important main course and returning contender Mark Froydenlund is banking on it being his strongest dish. Russell Bateman is creating a beef dish, and Ronnie Murrays mixing a crown of lamb with a curried filling.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the dessert course and the final chance for three of London and South Easts most competitive and talented chefs to get through to the judging chamber, today the veteran judge will send one chef home.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the head to head and the two highest scoring chefs from the London and South East region are cooking for judges Oliver Peyton, Prue Leith and Matthew Fort. Joining this week as guest judge is Kevin Gould, an award winning food writer.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Mini Patel is back for a second attempt at the north east regional title but he is competing against two young newcomers, Tommy Banks and Chris Archer. Last year, Mini went home after the dessert course, so he is hoping to keep better control of his nerves this year.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the fish course and two chefs, Tommy and Mini, have very similar ingredients - both are cooking mackerel. But Mini is trying an original beetroot sorbet while Tommy is using wild woodruff and oyster leaf from his own garden. Chris Archer has chosen the king of the sea, turbot, but there is controversy in the kitchen.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the main course of the north east region and overnight there has been a dramatic development. It is a tough and emotional day for the chefs as they react to the news but they battle on in a bid to present a perfect dish with venison and wagyu-angus beef - the meat of the day.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is a highly emotional day for one of the chefs as he prepares a dish which is inspired by and a tribute to his own great Briton, his late grandfather. But all is not going to plan and the chef ends up making one key element three times in a bid to achieve perfection.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two chefs are cooking for judges Matthew Fort, Prue Leith and Oliver Peyton for the very first time. Joining them as guest judge is John Williams MBE, the executive head chef at The Ritz, the only hotel to hold a royal warrant for banqueting.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is Chris McGowans third time however, he is facing tough competition from Mark Abbott, head chef at Daniel Cliffords 2 Michelin star restaurant, Midsummer House, and also from Eddie Attwell who has just completed a year as head chef at Ardtara Country House in Upperlands.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Mark Abbott is pushing himself again plating up a complex fish course of the very finest produce. He is calling it Shellsational but at the last minute he forgets a key element. Returning chef Chris McGowan is refining cod and chips and Eddie Attwell is getting classical with the king of the sea, turbot.The repartee is relentless as the chefs jostle for top place on the leader board. Once again there is plenty of emotion as the veteran chef delivers the scores.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the main course and returning Chef Chris McGowan is feeling confident but part of his dish involves making his own venison sausages to serve with a venison rack, and getting it all done in time is straining his emotions. Eddie Attwell is also serving the meat but with a traditional Northern Irish dish called Dock pudding. Mark Abbott is pushing himself again, he is using wagyu beef and serving it with tongue and corn cooked in a variety of ways.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The three chefs from Northern Ireland are cooking their desserts and one chef is on course for a historic score, he could beat his boss - a veteran of GBM - and achieve more marks than anyone has done throughout the history of the competition.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the head to head. The two highest scoring chefs from Northern Ireland are cooking for judges Oliver Peyton, Prue Leith and Matthew Fort who are joined by guest judge, restaurant critic and Editor-at-Large of the Independent, Amol Rajan.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the national finals of `Great British Menu. Before cooking starts the eight champions are called to Londons Southbank to get a sight of the prize, the chance to cook at a banquet held at the Palace of Westminster, in celebration of everyday Great Britons honoured by the Queen. It has a huge impact on every one of the chefs, each is determined to do all he can to get a dish to the banquet.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the national finals of `Great British Menu. Before cooking starts the eight champions are called to Londons Southbank to get a sight of the prize, the chance to cook at a banquet held at the Palace of Westminster, in celebration of everyday Great Britons honoured by the Queen. It has a huge impact on every one of the chefs, each is determined to do all he can to get a dish to the banquet.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its day two of the national finals, and the eight champions cook their fish courses. This years challenge has been to produce dishes that are a lasting legacy to the New Elizabethan Age. They must showcase the transformation in British cuisine during the Queens historic 64 year reign.
