programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: The Tunnels of Gibraltar were a Second World War espionage base, hidden in the Rock of Gibraltar, which lay undiscovered for decades. At Atlas-1 Test Site, are the remains of an ingenious US Airforce project that continues to keep people safe.

programme.title: Secrets of the London Underground
programme.desc: Tim Dunn and Siddy Holloway explore the abandoned branch line between Holborn and Aldwych. And at Holloway Road they find out about the Tubes only spiral escalator.

programme.title: Directors Short Cut
programme.desc: Bringing the best in television.

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: In the Scottish capital of Edinburgh, hidden from view, a series of vaults tell a murky story of opportunity, crime and destitution. At Fort Schwatka Unalaska are the remains of an Alaskan battleground, where a foreign invader set foot on American soil for the first time since 1812.

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: An Appalachian dream that conjured the spirit of the old west, left to rot on a North Carolina mountainside. In the ex-Soviet republic of Georgia are the remains of an ingenious sky-high solution to a ground-based problem, Chiatura, once known as the Venice of the air.

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: Exploring Agdam, a city in Azerbaijan raised to the ground in a conflict decided by a new weapon of war.

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: Explores an industrial ruin in the Florida Everglades in the south of the United States that once competed in the greatest race humankind has ever witnessed: the mission to land on the Moon.

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: Exploring traces of a mass deception hidden at Ponte Dirillo in Italy, as well as a once lavish theatre in Ohio and an ancient city with a poisonous secret.

programme.title: Secrets of the London Underground
programme.desc: Tim Dunn and Siddy Holloway explore the disused parts of Piccadilly Circus, the grandest station on the London Underground, and visit the long-abandoned Highgate surface station.

programme.title: Secrets of the London Underground
programme.desc: Tim Dunn and Siddy Holloway explore the hidden parts of Euston, including unique features not found elsewhere, They also examine the bits which are set for demolition to make way for HS2.

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: The owners of an excitable cocker spaniel hope Professor Noel Fitzpatrick can fix her leg problems by fitting two bionic back feet. Noel also launches a series of investigations into what is causing a cat to drag its leg.

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Professor Noel Fitzpatrick pushes the boundaries with a 10-month-old golden retriever who helps a five-year-old deal with her epilepsy. Noel uses groundbreaking cell technology to help repair the dogs damaged cartilage.

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: South Central paramedics race to a newborn baby with a low temperature, while crews in Oxford and Portsmouth deal with a man vomiting blood and a patients mysterious hip pain.

programme.title: 999: Rescue Squad
programme.desc: The team treat a lorry driver trapped behind his twisted steering wheel and help is urgently required for a 12-year-old who appears to be drunk after drinking an unknown substance.

programme.title: 999: Rescue Squad
programme.desc: The discovery of underwear on a canal bank sparks a risky missing persons search and a police incident ends in a fleeing car passenger jumping over a wall and breaking his leg.

programme.title: Gavin and Stacey
programme.desc: Stacey and Gavin return from honeymoon to a big welcome from both families. However, Smithy seems none too happy with his best mate and Nessa has a confession to make that throws the whole evening into disarray.

programme.title: Gavin and Stacey
programme.desc: Devastated by the news hes to become a father, Smithy goes AWOL. The boys finally track him down, but can he be persuaded to do the right thing? Stacey is shocked to hear of Gwens new domestic arrangements as the prospect of living in Essex finally hits her.

programme.title: Gavin and Stacey
programme.desc: Stacey is getting used to being an Essex wife, but its not as exciting as shed hoped, however, she bucks up at the weekend, when its time for a home visit to Barry. Meanwhile, Smithy goes with Nessa for the first scan and they get the first glimpse of their baby.

programme.title: DIY SOS: The Big Build Ireland
programme.desc: Baz Ashmawy and his team set out to renovate the homes of deserving families, changing their lives forever, but with only nine days to transform each home, and dependent on the generosity of volunteers, the pressure is truly on.

programme.desc: Sergeant Stone suspects that a shoplifter is the victim of domestic violence, but the woman refuses to make a statement against her husband.

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Two girls are caught fighting outside a school - an event which later leads to Smithy being stabbed.

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Deakin and Carver find their Friday night ebbing away as they tackle a particularly difficult robbery suspect. However, Keanes enthusiasm soon pays dividends and the pair find some buried treasure in a Chinese takeaway.

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: A girl is suspected of being raped at a party. The Sun Hill team link it to a break-in and an assault.

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: After a man is found with serious head injuries, a school pupil falls under suspicion.

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Nate and Mel are called to Deansgate School to investigate a suspected burglary in teacher Becky Jamess classroom. One of the suspects is pupil Kim Patterson - who admits that she and Jimmy Ryan broke into the school for a dare but did not do anything wrong. Then, Becky is found at her home, badly beaten and unconscious.

programme.title: 999: Rescue Squad
programme.desc: A teenager is found with multiple stab wounds in the street near his home, an amateur tree surgeon is badly hurt when a branch falls on his leg and two schoolgirls are run over.

programme.title: 999: Rescue Squad
programme.desc: The team treat a lorry driver trapped behind his twisted steering wheel and help is urgently required for a 12-year-old who appears to be drunk after drinking an unknown substance.

programme.title: 999: Rescue Squad
programme.desc: The discovery of underwear on a canal bank sparks a risky missing persons search and a police incident ends in a fleeing car passenger jumping over a wall and breaking his leg.

programme.title: 999: Rescue Squad
programme.desc: A major fire breaks out in a haulage depot in Brighouse and a serious accident at an illegal off-road biking site leads to a rider needing urgent hospital treatment.

programme.title: 999: Rescue Squad
programme.desc: A skier falls over on a steep slope and suffers a suspected brain injury in Wakefield and a DIY enthusiasts attempts to fix the heating in his chilly home end in disaster.

programme.title: 999: Rescue Squad
programme.desc: The start of the new school term leads of a spate of stabbings and a forester is badly injured in an accident involving a chainsaw deep in woods near Keighley.

programme.title: First Responders
programme.desc: The Auckland Rescue Helicopter crew are dispatched to a critical situation when a drag racer smashes into a concrete wall at 225kph.

programme.title: First Responders
programme.desc: Tomas discovers a man who has been shot on the mountain while hes having a guided tourist hike, but the man wont tell anyone whats happened. Åsa investigates the case with Tomas.

programme.title: Gavin and Stacey
programme.desc: Stacey and Gavin return from honeymoon to a big welcome from both families. However, Smithy seems none too happy with his best mate and Nessa has a confession to make that throws the whole evening into disarray.

programme.title: Gavin and Stacey
programme.desc: Devastated by the news hes to become a father, Smithy goes AWOL. The boys finally track him down, but can he be persuaded to do the right thing? Stacey is shocked to hear of Gwens new domestic arrangements as the prospect of living in Essex finally hits her.

programme.title: Gavin and Stacey
programme.desc: Stacey is getting used to being an Essex wife, but its not as exciting as shed hoped, however, she bucks up at the weekend, when its time for a home visit to Barry. Meanwhile, Smithy goes with Nessa for the first scan and they get the first glimpse of their baby.

programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show
programme.desc: The second series of the hilarious comedy sketch show begins. Characters this time include Lauren, Derek Faye, who may find someone impertinent, and Nan.

programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show
programme.desc: Characters include a rather unorthodox dog trainer whose methods produce some quite frankly hair-raising results.

programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show
programme.desc: Lauren goes to a gym, wigmakers to the stars Ivan and Trudy are introduced, and Derek gets offered tickets to the Gay Pride Parade and is offended.

programme.title: DIY SOS: The Big Build Ireland
programme.desc: Baz Ashmawy and his team set out to renovate the homes of deserving families, changing their lives forever, but with only nine days to transform each home, and dependent on the generosity of volunteers, the pressure is truly on.

programme.title: Louis Theroux Interviews...
programme.desc: Louis gets whisked away by adventurer and television presenter Bear Grylls to visit his private island, where he is staying for the summer with his wife Shara.

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys

programme.desc: Michael Portillo enters the Lake District via Ulverston, continues around the Furness Peninsula, and reaches the town of Barrow.

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.sub-title: North West and Isle of Man: Part 5
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his journey along the Cumbrian coast visiting Whitehaven, which from 1630 became the third most popular port in England.

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael arrives in Middlesbrough, the first town created by the railways when the Stockton to Darlington line was extended to the River Tees to create a new port.

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his railway adventure at Whitby, where he explores the ports shipbuilding and seafaring heritage. In Grosmont, Michael boards the first steam locomotive built in Britain since the 1960s. In the vast open landscape of the North York Moors, Michael discovers one of the most powerful military radars in the world - where tension rises as the team suspect a missile launch.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Dereks off to Staffordshire, to collect an unusual haul including a Blue Star Truck and a mysterious Lion Omnibus Reliant.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: A sun-bleached red Vauxhall Astra GTE gives the team a headache. They also buy a classic French moped and catch up on the restoration of a barn find Mini Cooper S.

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: Churchill described Italy as the `soft underbelly of the crocodile, thinking the Allies could cut through it to reach Germany. However, it turns out to be tougher than he imagined.

programme.title: Nazi Hunters
programme.desc: A look at the painstaking hunt for Nazi war criminals, beginning with the US Army task force in charge of locating brilliant German scientists.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The roadshow is at Swanage Pier and seafront in Dorset on Englands south coast, where treasures include a rare Omega watch that was run over by a lawnmower - but does expert Richard Price think its worth saving? Duncan Campbell is amazed by the best silver item hes ever seen on a roadshow, a tiny teapot by a renowned maker, while a group of three stunning diamond jewels makes John Benjamins day.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The roadshow is at Crystal Palace Park in London, where highlights include a diamond ring found wrapped up in a sock, a Louis Vuitton trunk, correspondence with a direct link to Haile Selassie, and a surprise visit from a living artist.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: Derek points the team towards one of his favourite motors - a 1983 Daimler Double Six. And a Triumph 2000 is given a modern day makeover under the bonnet.

programme.title: Lucy Worsley Investigates
programme.desc: Lucy Worsley investigates how the trial of one `witch sparked Britains witch-hunt craze. The story goes that in 1590, a coven of witches gathered here to cast a spell to try to kill the King of Scotland, James VI.

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys

programme.title: Dream Car Fixers

programme.title: Dream Car Fixers
programme.desc: Rupert has decided life is too short not to follow his dream of owning a Morris Traveller. Will Ruperts 13,000 pound budget stretch to a good one?

programme.title: Dream Car Fixers
programme.desc: Eric loves open-top motoring, but his sports car is too low and he needs an automatic car, so Sheldon and Peter suggest a VW Golf Cabriolet that might stretch Erics budget.

programme.title: Dream Car Fixers
programme.desc: Norman has dreamt of owning a Volvo 850R since watching Volvo British touring cars race, but fixers Sheldon and Peter could struggle to bring Normans dream to life.

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: The real side of boating with Robbie Cumming. On the last leg of his epic journey across the North of England, Robbie tackles the fast-flowing River Ouse and is forced to make an emergency stop. His destination is Ripon Basin at the outer reaches of our inland waterways.

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming begins his 300-mile boating adventure across the north of England and the Midlands. He is forced to make a running repair to his narrowboat, the Naughty Lass, while on the Tinsley lock flight in South Yorkshire, he cruises beneath Conisbroughs magnificent viaduct and must wait for an unusual sliding railway bridge to open on the Stainforth and Keadby Canal.

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming tackles the fast-flowing tidal River Trent and is invited to join a night-time illuminated boat parade. However, his journey is hampered by a damaged wall in Worksop and a water shortage on the picturesque Chesterfield Canal.

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Autumnal fog and an overheating engine prove to be problems for narrowboater Robbie Cumming as he navigates a Roman waterway and the fast-flowing River Trent. Its a journey of many challenges as he runs aground and breaks down in Nottingham.

programme.title: Tropic of Cancer
programme.desc: Simon rejoins the Tropic in Egypt and follows it through Saudi Arabia, Dubai, and Oman. In Southern Egypt, next to the ancient temple of Abu Simbal, Simon meets Nubians struggling to maintain their culture following the damming of the Nile and dives in the pristine reefs of the Red Sea.

programme.title: Simon Reeves South America
programme.desc: Simon travels from Chiles Atacama Desert right down to Tierra Del Fuego, the most southerly inhabited place on Earth. The Atacama, one of the driest places on the planet, has as little as 1mm of rain per year.

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: Series celebrating our planet in all its glory. This episode journeys across the world, following the influence of the sun and discovering how its seasonal journey affects the lives of all who live on earth. As spring arrives in the Arctic, a mother polar bear emerges from her den with two tiny cubs. At the other end of the planet, winter arrives and Emperor penguins are plunged into darkness for four months.

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: Taking an epic journey following the descent of the planets mightiest rivers from their mountain sources to the sea. Breathtaking river scenery is revealed by a revolutionary helicopter camera mount - from the worlds highest and broadest waterfalls to the awe-inspiring spectacle of the 10,000-strong flocks of greater snow geese in flight.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Jacks baby Max makes his podium debut with Great Grandad Derek. Paul visits a former stock car racer and finds a Pinto pimped Anglia and a Talbot Sunbeam Lotus.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Paul collects a cherished 1924 Rover 9 thats been in the same family since the 1950s and only had one previous owner before that.

programme.title: Train Truckers

programme.title: Train Truckers

programme.title: Bangers and Cash

programme.title: Bangers and Cash

programme.title: Bangers and Cash

programme.title: Lamhe
programme.desc: Viren falls in love at first sight when he sees the beautiful Pallavi, but is too shy to let her know his feelings. He then realises she is in love with Siddharth, whom she goes on to marry. When Pallavi and Siddharth die in a car crash, Viren takes charge of their daughter Pooja. As the years pass, Viren and Pooja fall in love, but Viren is not prepared to accept his feelings for a girl so much younger than himself.

programme.title: Yamla Pagla Deewana
programme.desc: A married Canadian travels to India to re-unite with his estranged father and brother but faces obstacles and challenges.

programme.title: Band Baaja Baaraat

programme.title: Laal Singh Chaddha

programme.title: Jigra
programme.desc: A young woman with a troubled past embarks on a dangerous mission. Her goal is to help her younger brother, who is imprisoned in a foreign jail.

programme.title: Shootout at Lokhandwala
programme.desc: On a calm summer day in 1991, in the bustling Lokhandwala Complex, five criminals including Maya and Dilip were counting 70 lakhs in flat No 32B, when 286 policemen, headed by ACP Khan, took strategic positions around their building. A gunfire ensued and the entire nation witnessed the most talked about daylight encounter lasting 6 hours that transformed suburban Mumbai into a virtual war zone.

programme.title: Joggers Park
programme.desc: Justice Jatin Chatterjee, a retired judge, meets Jenny, a glamorous hotel executive and model, while walking in the park. Jenny has a sad history in love, and is happy single. Mr Chatterjee is a well-respected celebrity who believes one shouldnt `fall in love but `rise in it. At first Jenny isnt interested and their relationship develops first as friendship, but as it changes, the judge is afraid of the consequences.

programme.title: Aa Ab Laut Chalen
programme.desc: In a situation remarkably similar to present-day Britain, graduates in 1990s India found that education was no guarantee of employment. Unable to get work in Delhi, Rohan decides to try his luck in New York. When he gets there, he finds himself homeless, jobless and desperate. Two taxi drivers - an Indian and a Pakistani - take him in and help him find work. He meets Pooja, who soon develops feelings for him.

programme.title: The Who Bhi Din

programme.desc: Instead of coming out to their families a gay man and a lesbian enter into a marriage of convenience to appease their parents. However chaos soon ensues when the womans girlfriend comes to live with the unlikely couple.

programme.desc: Indian and Chinese forces clash along the Sikkim border in the aftermath of the 1962 Sino-Indian War.

programme.title: Uri: The Surgical Strike
